iia_vs_random_subspace_null.interpretation: Real IIA should be significantly above random-subspace null (p < 0.05).

alpha_vs_iia.interpretation: Positive rho = low-dim (high-alpha) regions have higher IIA. Bootstrap CI and permutation p provide non-parametric guarantees.

iia_by_geometric_type.interpretation: Step function: high-alpha regions should have IIA > 0.7, low-alpha < 0.4. Kruskal-Wallis tests group differences.

alpha_vs_ev_prior_separation.interpretation: Negative rho = high-dim regions keep evidence and prior in separate subspaces. Positive rho = low-dim regions merge evidence and prior.

alpha_vs_excess_iia.interpretation: Correlation with IIA minus random-subspace null. Ensures result isn't a dimensionality artifact.

causal_graph_stats.interpretation: Mean asymmetry > 0 means the causal graph is directional, not symmetric. High asymmetry implies genuine causal flow, not just shared information.
